_meta.name: Sample ACR-AC Knowledge Base
_meta.description: Synthetic, illustrative seed data structured to mirror the ACR Appropriateness Criteria. NOT licensed ACR content. For research/development use only.

rrl_legend.high: Moderate (1-10 mSv, e.g. head CT, abdominal CT)

variants.scenario: Sudden severe headache, peak intensity within seconds to minutes ("thunderclap" or "worst headache of life"). Initial imaging.

procedures.comment: First-line for evaluation of acute subarachnoid hemorrhage.

procedures.procedure: CTA head and neck with IV contrast
procedures.comment: If aneurysm or vascular cause suspected, often performed alongside non-contrast CT.

procedures.procedure: MRI head without and with IV contrast
procedures.comment: Useful when CT non-diagnostic and clinical concern persists.
Alternative vascular imaging when CTA contraindicated.
Reserved for cases requiring intervention or when noninvasive imaging is equivocal.
Not indicated.

variants.scenario: Chronic headache, no red flags, no neurologic deficit. Initial imaging.

procedures.comment: Generally not indicated absent red flags; consider if pattern changes.
Not indicated for primary chronic headache.
Clinical management appropriate; imaging not indicated without red flags.

variants.scenario: New headache with focal neurologic deficit, papilledema, or immunocompromised state.

procedures.procedure: MRI head without and with IV contrast
procedures.comment: Preferred for evaluation of mass, infection, demyelination.
Useful when MRI unavailable or for rapid screening.
Reasonable alternative when MRI contraindicated.

variants.scenario: Acute low back pain, no red flags, less than 6 weeks duration, no prior trauma.

procedures.comment: Conservative management is first-line; imaging not indicated.
Not indicated in absence of red flags.

procedures.procedure: MRI lumbar spine without IV contrast
procedures.comment: Reserve for persistent symptoms or red flags.

variants.scenario: Low back pain with red flags suggesting cauda equina, infection, or malignancy (e.g. saddle anesthesia, fever, unexplained weight loss, history of cancer).

procedures.procedure: MRI lumbar spine without and with IV contrast
procedures.comment: Preferred for infection, malignancy, cauda equina evaluation.

procedures.procedure: MRI lumbar spine without IV contrast
procedures.comment: Sufficient if contrast contraindicated.

procedures.procedure: CT lumbar spine without IV contrast
procedures.comment: Alternative if MRI contraindicated.
Limited role; may screen for fracture or alignment.

variants.scenario: Acute chest pain, low to intermediate probability of acute coronary syndrome, normal or non-diagnostic ECG and negative initial troponin.

procedures.procedure: CTA coronary arteries with IV contrast
procedures.comment: High negative predictive value; appropriate for low-intermediate risk rule-out.
Functional alternative; no ionizing radiation.
Established functional imaging.
Useful when other modalities equivocal.
Helpful to exclude alternative causes of chest pain.

variants.scenario: Acute chest pain, high clinical probability of ACS or evolving MI on ECG.

procedures.comment: Definitive evaluation; allows immediate intervention.

procedures.procedure: CTA coronary arteries with IV contrast
procedures.comment: Not appropriate when invasive angiography indicated.

topics.topic_name: Right Lower Quadrant Pain — Suspected Appendicitis

variants.scenario: Adult, suspected acute appendicitis. Initial imaging.

procedures.procedure: CT abdomen and pelvis with IV contrast
procedures.comment: First-line in adults; high sensitivity and specificity.
Operator-dependent; preferred if radiation avoidance is a priority.

procedures.procedure: MRI abdomen and pelvis without and with IV contrast
procedures.comment: Useful when CT contraindicated.
Low yield for appendicitis.
First-line in pregnancy; no ionizing radiation.

procedures.procedure: MRI abdomen and pelvis without IV contrast
procedures.comment: Strong second-line when US non-diagnostic.

procedures.procedure: CT abdomen and pelvis with IV contrast
procedures.comment: Reserved for diagnostic uncertainty after US/MRI.
First-line in children to avoid radiation.

procedures.procedure: MRI abdomen and pelvis without IV contrast
procedures.comment: Second-line when US non-diagnostic.

procedures.procedure: CT abdomen and pelvis with IV contrast
procedures.comment: Used selectively given radiation considerations.

variants.scenario: Suspected PE, intermediate or high pretest probability, normal renal function.

procedures.procedure: CTA chest with IV contrast (PE protocol)
procedures.comment: Test of choice; sensitive and specific for PE.
Alternative when iodinated contrast contraindicated.
Limited availability and expertise required.
Often performed for alternative diagnoses; non-diagnostic for PE.
Indirect evidence via DVT; may be used when CTA contraindicated.
Reasonable initial step if DVT clinically suspected.

procedures.procedure: CTA chest with IV contrast (PE protocol)
procedures.comment: Diagnostic; fetal radiation low. Use when clinical suspicion warrants.
Alternative; comparable diagnostic performance.
Helps select between V/Q and CTA.
